Speaker 1: All right, guys. Welcome to the final themeplate of Module 3. So this is just going to be a
small twist of the themeplate we explored earlier, except that milestone moment that
we're reconfirming is the webinar registration. So, even though someone registered for
your webinar, it's super crucial to recognize and realize that it does not guarantee a
show up, and while it does vary across industry, you'd usually be happy and lucky if 25%
of your sign-ups show up live, which is kind of problematic when you think about it. You
know you're getting those leads, sometimes from Facebook traffic that you're paying
for. So why would you pay for someone to register for a webinar if you weren't willing
to invest a little bit of an extra effort to sell the show up, coach the show ups, since that
is going to be so critical and essential for the final conversion?
So this themeplate is all about two things, selling the show up and preframing the sale,
and like its earlier version, it does boil down to those five C's, confidence and
reassurance around that decision, confirming what it means about them, adding clarity
around outcomes they can expect, building curiosity around the way the outcome will
be delivered, and credibility that you can deliver it.
So this is the container at a glance. We're gonna congratulate them on signing up for the
webinar, confirm to them that this is a bigger deal than they may have initially realized.
Especially important here since most people aren't too emotionally invested in webinar
sign-ups anymore. Diagnose what this tells you about them. You know, these are the
positive traits that you're gonna get to reflect back to them and hold them consistent
with later.
Remind them again that this is a big deal. State why it's a big deal. You've gotta quantify
it, qualify it, and tie it to your big promise or sales argument. Tell them that they'll be
rewarded for that action. You know, that's a very encouraging thing that brings people
along and remind them of the big promise. You know, the result that they'll get when
they attend. What pains, fears, frustrations they'll no longer have to deal with and what
pleasures, desires they'll soon experience as the new normal. Once again, credibility
bomb of why they can trust you to deliver it. Then sign off and you can just close with
those webinar details.
So, all of that said, here is an example. Boom, you're in. Here's what this says about you.
So once again, it's that bit of a pattern interrupt here going on and just that diagnostic,
you know. People are very curious. Oh, you're gonna be talking about me? That kind of
perks the ears up and cues them in and they kind of have to see what it's all about. So,
you're in, first name, and I don't say that lightly. This is particularly important here
because, once again, webinars don't have that significance that it once had. For a single
click you just made the bold, irrevocable decision that your company's profits and
growth will never be held hostage by bad copy or overbooked, overpriced copywriters
again.

confirmations. You know, I use to have this terrible habit of collecting webinars. So
you're not alienating them or shaming them in, just kind of like that benign sign up. You
know, or that meaningless sign. Because, hey, I do it too. You know, signing up for them
just in case without any actual intention of showing up. So I know it's a thing and I know
there are some amongst us. I ain't naming names. Most of you are solid. But if you're a
just-in-caser, consider this your lucky day. This is that one case that you'll be thanking
yourself for later. Because what you learn in these 90 minutes can quite literally impact
every aspect of your business moving forward. We cool? Cool.
So, that's what we're doing in the P.P.S. We're selling them against this behavior that we
don't want, this just-in-case behavior. And we're kind of poking fun at it. We're saying
that we're kind of like one of those people too, but we're still talking about it kind of in a
negative light. You don't want to be the just-in-caser, you know. And if you were, this is
your chance to no longer be that just-in-caser. So this comes down to always knowing
what you need to coach and in this case, after someone signed up for a webinar, you
absolutely want to coach the attendants.

Along with these themeplates, I suggest you answer the questions, first of all, earlier in
the workbook and then spend anywhere between ... I like to set a sand timer for like 20
minutes, 25 minutes to get out a first draft. I don't edit when I write. And then just kind
of bang them out. You know, you could get these done in literally one coffee. Grande,
venti. Go venti just in case. I don't wanna have to backtrack on a promise, but this is
meant to be written quickly and these containers and the questions you answered
before are meant to really got it and help you do it as quickly and as effectively as
possible. Confident in knowing that you're hitting the right triggers at the right times.
So, that's all for Module 3. Get this done. Take a break. Don't dive into Module 4 until
you feel ready and like you want more. So, take the time you need and I'll meet you in
Module 4 when you are ready.
